content: Real-world conflict detection at scale uses spatial data structures (R-trees, octree grids) to avoid O(n^2) pairwise checks because pairwise comparison doesn't scale to hundreds/thousands of aircraft. For a single-airport sim with a handful of aircraft (single digits to low tens), naive O(n^2) pairwise separation checking every sim tick is computationally trivial and is the correct choice - spatial partitioning is premature optimization at this scale.
